metadata.title: Dalyan Turkey - Official Tourism Guide | Boat Tours, Ancient Sites & Iztuzu Beach
metadata.description: Discover Dalyan, Turkey's hidden paradise. Explore ancient Kaunos ruins, Lycian Rock Tombs, pristine Iztuzu Turtle Beach, relaxing mud baths, and unforgettable boat tours along the Dalyan River.
metadata.keywords: Dalyan Turkey, Dalyan tourism, Iztuzu Beach, Kaunos ancient city, Lycian Rock Tombs, Dalyan boat tours, mud baths, Caretta Caretta, Dalyan River, Turkey travel

hero.title: Welcome to Dalyan
hero.subtitle: Turkey's Hidden Paradise
hero.description: Where ancient history meets untouched nature. Discover the magical Dalyan River, thousand-year-old rock tombs, pristine turtle beaches, and healing mud baths.

home.featuredSubtitle: A timeless destination where nature, history, and adventure converge

ancientSitesCard.title: Ancient Sites
ancientSitesCard.description: Explore the 3,000-year-old Kaunos Ancient City and the magnificent Lycian Rock Tombs carved into cliffsides.

boatToursCard.title: Boat Tours
boatToursCard.description: Cruise along the Dalyan River through reed-lined channels to discover hidden coves and natural wonders.

iztuuzCard.title: Iztuzu Beach
iztuuzCard.description: Visit one of Europe's finest beaches and nesting ground of the endangered Caretta Caretta sea turtles.

mudBathsCard.title: Mud Baths
mudBathsCard.description: Rejuvenate in natural thermal mud baths and sulphur springs known for their healing properties.

home.whyDescription: Dalyan is one of Turkey's most unique destinations, offering a perfect blend of ancient history, pristine nature, and authentic Turkish culture. Unlike crowded tourist resorts, Dalyan preserves its natural charm and ecological treasures.

t1.text: Dalyan is the most beautiful place I've ever visited. The boat ride through the river with the rock tombs in the background was breathtaking.
t1.author: Sarah M.

t2.text: The mud baths were so much fun, and Iztuzu Beach is absolutely stunning. A must-visit destination in Turkey.
t2.author: Hans W.

t3.text: We spent a week in Dalyan and it wasn't enough. The history, nature, and friendly people made it unforgettable.
t3.author: Marie L.

ancientSites.title: Ancient Sites & Historical Treasures
ancientSites.subtitle: Step back in time and explore millennia of civilizations that flourished in the Dalyan region

kaunos.title: Kaunos Ancient City
kaunos.description: Kaunos is one of the most important ancient cities in the Caria region, founded around the 10th century BC. Situated on a dramatic hilltop overlooking the Dalyan River, this archaeological site reveals layers of civilizations including Carian, Greek, Roman, and Byzantine periods.
kaunos.highlights: The well-preserved Roman theatre with seating for 5,000 spectators, the ancient harbour (now a lake), the acropolis with panoramic views, Roman baths, Byzantine basilica, and impressive city walls stretching along the hillside.
kaunos.visiting: Open daily. Accessible by boat from Dalyan town center (15 minutes) or by road. Allow 2-3 hours for a thorough visit. Bring water and comfortable walking shoes.
kaunos.tip: Visit in the early morning or late afternoon to avoid the heat and enjoy golden light on the ruins.

rockTombs.title: Lycian Rock Tombs
rockTombs.description: The magnificent Lycian Rock Tombs of Dalyan are carved directly into the sheer cliff face overlooking the Dalyan River. Dating back to the 4th century BC, these temple-style tombs were created for Kaunos' elite citizens and represent some of the finest examples of Lycian funerary architecture.
rockTombs.highlights: Six major tomb facades carved in Ionic temple style, dramatic cliff-face location visible from the river, best viewed at sunset when they glow golden, illuminated at night creating a magical atmosphere.
rockTombs.visiting: Best viewed from a boat on the Dalyan River or from Dalyan town's riverside promenade. The tombs are illuminated after dark for stunning nighttime viewing.
rockTombs.tip: Take an evening boat ride to see the tombs illuminated against the night sky — an unforgettable experience.

canal.title: Dalyan Canal & Ancient Waterway
canal.description: The Dalyan Canal is a 12-kilometer natural waterway connecting Lake Köyceğiz to the Mediterranean Sea. This ancient trade route was used by civilizations for thousands of years and today offers one of Turkey's most scenic boat journeys through reed-lined channels teeming with wildlife.
canal.highlights: 12 km of winding waterways through pristine wetlands, rich bird life including kingfishers and herons, views of the Rock Tombs from the water, connection to both Lake Köyceğiz and the Mediterranean.
canal.visiting: Regular boat services run between Dalyan town, Kaunos, Iztuzu Beach, and Lake Köyceğiz. The canal is the main transportation artery of the region.
canal.tip: A sunrise boat ride through the canal offers the best wildlife viewing and photography opportunities.

boatTours.title: Boat Tours & Excursions
boatTours.subtitle: Experience Dalyan from the water — the way it was meant to be explored

river.title: Dalyan River Classic Tour
river.description: The signature Dalyan experience. Cruise along the beautiful Dalyan River, visit the ancient city of Kaunos, marvel at the Lycian Rock Tombs, enjoy natural mud baths and thermal springs, and end the day at the magnificent Iztuzu Beach.
river.included: Boat transportation, Kaunos entrance fee, mud baths access, lunch on board, soft drinks, snorkelling equipment
river.highlights: Dalyan River cruise through reed channels, Lycian Rock Tombs viewing, Kaunos Ancient City visit, Natural mud baths and sulphur springs, Iztuzu Turtle Beach, Swimming stops in crystal-clear waters

turtle.title: Turtle Beach Express
turtle.description: A shorter excursion perfect for those who want to visit the famous Iztuzu Beach by boat. Enjoy a scenic river cruise, spot Caretta Caretta turtles in their natural habitat, and spend time on one of Europe's finest beaches.
turtle.included: Boat transportation, 2 hours beach time, soft drinks, turtle spotting guide
turtle.highlights: Scenic Dalyan River cruise, Caretta Caretta turtle spotting, 4.5 km pristine sandy beach, Crystal-clear Mediterranean waters, Views of Lycian Rock Tombs en route

mud.title: Mud Bath & Thermal Springs Tour
mud.description: Discover the famous Dalyan mud baths and thermal sulphur springs renowned for their rejuvenating and healing properties. Coat yourself in mineral-rich mud, dry in the sun, then rinse off in hot thermal pools.
mud.included: Boat transportation, mud baths access, thermal springs access, towels, refreshments
mud.highlights: Natural mineral-rich mud pools, Hot sulphur thermal springs (39°C), Skin rejuvenation treatment, Relaxing river cruise, Beautiful natural surroundings

moonlight.title: Moonlight River Cruise
moonlight.description: A magical evening cruise along the Dalyan River under the stars. See the Lycian Rock Tombs beautifully illuminated against the night sky, enjoy dinner on board, and experience the tranquil beauty of Dalyan after dark.
moonlight.included: Evening boat cruise, dinner on board, drinks, live music (seasonal), illuminated tombs viewing
moonlight.highlights: Illuminated Lycian Rock Tombs, Starlit river cruise, Dinner on board with local cuisine, Live Turkish music (seasonal), Magical evening atmosphere

discovery.title: Full-Day Dalyan Discovery
discovery.description: The ultimate Dalyan experience combining all major attractions in one comprehensive tour. Visit Kaunos Ancient City, the Rock Tombs, mud baths, thermal springs, Lake Köyceğiz, and Iztuzu Beach — perfect for first-time visitors.
discovery.included: All entrance fees, lunch and drinks, mud baths, thermal springs, snorkelling gear, professional guide
discovery.highlights: Complete Dalyan experience in one day, Kaunos Ancient City guided tour, Lycian Rock Tombs, Natural mud baths and thermal springs, Lake Köyceğiz, Iztuzu Turtle Beach, Professional English-speaking guide

iztuzu.title: Iztuzu Beach — Turtle Beach
iztuzu.subtitle: One of Europe's finest beaches and a critical nesting ground for the endangered Caretta Caretta sea turtles

overview.title: Beach Overview
overview.description: Iztuzu Beach, also known as Turtle Beach, is a stunning 4.5 km stretch of golden sand that forms a natural barrier between the Dalyan River Delta and the Mediterranean Sea. This unique ecosystem is one of the last major nesting sites for the Caretta Caretta (Loggerhead) sea turtles in the Mediterranean.

turtles.title: Caretta Caretta Sea Turtles
turtles.description: Every summer from May to October, endangered Loggerhead sea turtles return to Iztuzu Beach to lay their eggs. The beach has been a protected area since 1988 after an international campaign to save it from hotel development. Today, conservation efforts ensure that both turtles and visitors can enjoy this remarkable beach.

gettingThere.title: How to Get There
gettingThere.byBoat: By Boat: Regular boat services depart from Dalyan town center. The scenic 30-minute river cruise to the beach is an experience in itself, passing through reed-lined channels with views of the Rock Tombs.
gettingThere.byRoad: By Road: A paved road leads to the beach from the D400 highway. Parking available at the beach entrance. The road approach arrives at the river-side of the beach.
gettingThere.byMinibus: By Minibus: Regular dolmuş (minibus) services run from Dalyan town center to the beach during summer season.

rules.title: Beach Rules & Conservation
rules.r1: No umbrellas or sun loungers in the turtle nesting zone (marked area)
rules.r2: Beach closed from 20:00 to 08:00 during nesting season (May-October)
rules.r3: No flash photography on the beach at night
rules.r5: Keep the beach clean — take your litter with you
rules.r6: Do not disturb nesting turtles or hatchlings

facilities.title: Beach Facilities
facilities.items: Sun loungers and umbrellas (outside nesting zone), Changing rooms and showers, Beach restaurant and café, Lifeguard on duty, First aid station, Accessible wooden walkways

map.title: Interactive Tourism Map
map.subtitle: Explore all of Dalyan's attractions, tours, and points of interest

about.title: About Dalyan
about.subtitle: A charming riverside town nestled between mountains and sea in southwestern Turkey
about.intro: Dalyan is a picturesque town in Muğla Province, southwestern Turkey, situated along the banks of the Dalyan River between Lake Köyceğiz and the Mediterranean Sea. Known for its natural beauty, rich history, and ecological importance, Dalyan offers a refreshingly authentic Turkish experience away from mass tourism.

geography.title: Geography & Climate
geography.description: Dalyan enjoys a Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters. The region is characterized by its unique river delta ecosystem, surrounded by pine-covered mountains. Summer temperatures range from 30-40°C, while winters are mild at 10-15°C. The swimming season extends from May to November.

gettingHere.title: How to Get to Dalyan
gettingHere.airport: Dalaman Airport (DLM) is the nearest airport, just 25 km away (30 minutes by car). Regular international flights connect Dalaman to major European cities.
gettingHere.bus: Regular bus services connect Dalyan to nearby cities including Fethiye (1 hour), Marmaris (1.5 hours), and Bodrum (3 hours).
gettingHere.car: Dalyan is accessible via the D400 highway. Well-signed roads lead from the highway to the town center.

culture.title: Culture & Cuisine
culture.description: Dalyan offers authentic Turkish cuisine with an emphasis on fresh seafood, especially the famous Dalyan sea bass (levrek). The riverside restaurants serve traditional mezes, grilled fish, and regional specialities. The weekly Tuesday market is perfect for fresh local produce, spices, and handcrafts.

accommodation.title: Accommodation
accommodation.description: Dalyan offers a range of accommodation from boutique hotels and riverside pensions to luxury villas and apart-hotels. Most accommodation is within walking distance of the town center and river boat departures.

footer.description: Your complete guide to Dalyan, Turkey — one of the Mediterranean's most enchanting destinations.
